Tests the behavior of the Function constructor with duplicate parameter names.

# Purpose
This code is a short script that tests the behavior of the JavaScript `Function` constructor when given duplicate parameter names. It attempts to create a new function with two parameters both named `a` and a body that simply returns. The script is wrapped in a `try-catch` block to handle any exceptions that occur during the function creation. If an exception is caught, the `assert` function is called with `false`, indicating that the test has failed. This script is likely used to verify compliance with JavaScript standards regarding function parameter naming.
